title: Magnetic Sorting Challenge
instructions_for_student: Drag each material card into the correct bin. Think about whether each material is magnetic or non-magnetic to decide if it blocks or passes the magnetic effect.

additionalProps.title: Magnetic Sorting Challenge
additionalProps.subtitle: Drag each material into the correct bin

cards.label: Wood
cards.reason: Wood is non-magnetic, so the magnetic effect passes through it.

cards.label: Glass
cards.reason: Glass is non-magnetic, so the magnetic effect passes through it.

cards.label: Iron Sheet
cards.reason: Iron is a magnetic material — it absorbs and blocks the magnetic effect.

cards.label: Cardboard
cards.reason: Cardboard is non-magnetic, so the magnetic effect passes through it.

cards.label: Plastic
cards.reason: Plastic is non-magnetic, so the magnetic effect passes through it.

cards.label: Steel Plate
cards.reason: Steel contains iron and is magnetic — it absorbs and blocks the magnetic effect.

cards.label: Rubber
cards.reason: Rubber is non-magnetic, so the magnetic effect passes through it.

cards.label: Aluminium Foil
cards.reason: Aluminium is non-magnetic, so the magnetic effect passes through it.

_tool_meta.subject: Science
_tool_meta.activity_reference: Activity 4.7: Let us investigate — Effect of magnet through non-magnetic materials (Table 4.2)
_tool_meta.icons: inline SVG only — no lucide-react or external icon libraries

sorting_logic.blocks_bin: magnetic materials only — iron, steel, nickel, cobalt (and their alloys)
sorting_logic.noblock_bin: all non-magnetic materials — wood, glass, cardboard, plastic, rubber, aluminium, etc.

agent_readme.description: A two-bin drag-and-drop sorting tool. Cards start shuffled in a pool below the two bins. Students drag each material card into 'Blocks magnetic effect' (magnetic materials: iron, steel) or 'Does NOT block magnetic effect' (all others). Correct placements lock green and reveal a one-line reason. Wrong placements bounce back with a hint asking whether the material is magnetic. A completion modal shows all sorted items with reasons and the key insight.

subtitle.default: Drag each material into the correct bin

noblockBinLabel.description: Label for the 'does not block' bin

completionMessage.description: Heading shown on the completion modal

cards.description: Array of material cards. Must have at least 2 cards; recommended 6–10 for classroom use.

bin.description: 'blocks' for magnetic materials; 'noblock' for non-magnetic materials

reason.description: One-line explanation shown after correct placement

cards.label: Iron Nail
cards.reason: Iron is magnetic — it blocks the effect.

cards.label: Wooden Plank
cards.reason: Wood is non-magnetic — effect passes through.

cards.label: Steel Spoon
cards.reason: Steel is magnetic — it blocks the effect.

cards.label: Paper Sheet
cards.reason: Paper is non-magnetic — effect passes through.

decision_tree.standard_8_cards: Use the default cards array — covers the exact 8 materials from Activity 4.7 (Table 4.2) of NCERT Grade 6 Science Chapter 4
decision_tree.fewer_cards: Reduce the cards array to any number ≥ 2
decision_tree.add_nickel_cobalt: Add nickel/cobalt cards with bin='blocks' for extended learning
decision_tree.custom_topic: Replace all card objects with new materials for a different sorting activity (keep bin field as 'blocks' or 'noblock')
